brand_voice: Premium but not sterile. Honest, direct, and educational. Design-aware. Anti-pressure. Anti-generic-contractor. Intersects Bentley, punk rock, and posh art student.

rules.h1: text-5xl md:text-6xl tracking-tighter uppercase font-medium text-[#ededed]
rules.h2: text-3xl md:text-4xl tracking-tight uppercase font-medium text-[#ededed]
rules.general: Upper and lower casing used deliberately. Medium and heavy weights for hierarchy. Rarely use bold. No drop-shadow text.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Editorial, asymmetric, strong whitespace, magazine-style. Avoid generic SaaS cards and rounded heavy layouts.
spacing_and_layout.grid_system: Use pure HTML/Tailwind CSS grid. grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 with generous gaps (gap-8 to gap-16).
spacing_and_layout.containers: Use px-6 md:px-12 lg:px-24 py-20 md:py-32 for sections.

visual_details.textures: Analogue editorial details sparingly: subtle halftone dots, slight grain overprint, tasteful collage accents. Implemented via CSS background patterns or opacity layers.
visual_details.borders: 1px solid borders using border-[#ededed]/10 for structure instead of shadows.
visual_details.shadows: DO NOT USE drop shadows. Flat, rectangular, and dense styling.
visual_details.overlays: Hero images must use a gradient overlay (e.g., bg-gradient-to-t from-[#111111] via-[#111111]/80 to-transparent) to ensure text readability without killing the image.

sections.name: 1. Hero
sections.instructions: Full-bleed background image with heavy bottom gradient to merge into #111111. Eyebrow: 'Indianapolis Metro · Western Chicago Suburbs'. H1: 'Because the details matter.' Lead paragraph. Two CTAs (Primary: 'Start with your project', Secondary: 'See real cost ranges'). Trust signals row below CTAs.

sections.name: 2. Direct Answer Intro
sections.instructions: AEO/answer-engine-extractable paragraph. Large editorial text (text-2xl or text-3xl). Asymmetrical layout, off-centered. Focus on why the exterior decision matters.

sections.name: 3. What We Do
sections.instructions: Six service systems: Roofing, Windows & Doors, Siding, Gutters & Gutter Guards, Insurance Claims, Commercial Exterior Work. Layout as an architectural list or editorial grid (not standard SaaS cards). Each block explains 'what the decision really is'.

sections.name: 4. Real Cost Ranges
sections.instructions: Data section presenting median costs from 1,317 estimates. Use an editorial table or stat layout (NOT SaaS cards). Typography-driven numbers. Clear labeling that these are planning ranges, not quotes.
components: Custom HTML/Tailwind grid or heavily customized Shadcn Table

sections.name: 5. How We Work
sections.instructions: 5 steps: Listen first, Inspect the condition, Specify the system, Price the real scope, Manage the work. Vertical timeline or horizontal scrolling typographic list.

sections.name: 6. Founder / Ethos
sections.instructions: Story of Jon Strand. Image of artisan/craftsmanship. Text focus on Herron School of Art background, craft + design eye. Magazine article spread layout.

sections.name: 7. Project Proof
sections.instructions: Real stories (Caleb, Jay). Use asymmetrical image + text overlapping to feel like a premium editorial. High contrast, authentic photography.

sections.name: 8. Where We Work
sections.instructions: Lists of Indiana cities + Illinois cities. Typography-led multi-column list. Very clean, architectural alignment.

sections.name: 9. Common Questions
sections.instructions: FAQ accordion. 8 questions matching FAQPage schema. Minimalist borders (border-b only), no background cards.

sections.name: 10. Final CTA
sections.instructions: Start with the question that matters most. Massive typography. Two buttons. High contrast.

sections.name: 11. Footer
sections.instructions: Visible NAP (addresses, phone, email, hours). Very clean grid, architectural spacing. Links to future routes.

components_and_routes.navigation: Desktop + mobile nav. Fixed or sticky header, blending with background (solid #111111 or dark blur). Links: /roofing/, /windows-doors/, /siding/, /gutters/, /cost-scope-index/, /service-areas/, /project-proof/, /start-here/.
components_and_routes.buttons: Primary CTA: bg-[#ededed] text-[#111111] hover:bg-[#c47b4f] hover:text-[#ededed] transition-colors. Secondary CTA: border border-[#ededed] text-[#ededed] hover:bg-[#ededed]/10 transition-colors.

instructions_to_main_agent: Do NOT use emojis, em dashes, or generic contractor language.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute (e.g., data-testid='hero-primary-cta').
Do not use Shadcn 'Card' components for layout. Rely on pure HTML/Tailwind to build architectural, flat, dense grids.
Adhere strictly to colors #111111, #151515, and #ededed. Avoid all purple/blue SaaS gradients or any drop-shadows on text/cards.
Implement the custom font stack for display headers (Termina/Cabinet Grotesk) and body text (Inter) in tailwind.config.js.
Keep animations very subtle (fade in, slow translate) to preserve page speed and editorial feel.
Use <hr> or 1px border-t border-[#27272A] to define separation between sections instead of heavy drop shadows.
Ensure typography hierarchy is flawless. Huge headings must be offset by lots of negative space.
Do not invent fake reviews or numbers. Use the exact data from the problem statement.
